title: "linux keylogger(LKL)"

### Post by karthick87 on 2010-02-03
I have installed LKL(Linux Keylogger) but can anyone say how to run this?
I have done the following but am receiving an error..

karthick@Learners-desktop:~/Desktop/LKL$ sudo lkl -l -k us_km -o log.file

Started to log port 0x60. Keymap is us_km. The logfile is log.file.

unable to find keymap-file: No such file or directory
a keymap is required!! run lkl with -k <keymap>

---

### Post by karthick87 on 2010-02-03
I have tried this but it shows Segmentation fault...

karthick@Learners-desktop:~$ sudo lkl -l -k /usr/share/lkl/keymaps/us_km -o log.txt

Started to log port 0x60. Keymap is /usr/share/lkl/keymaps/us_km. The logfile is log.txt.
Segmentation fault

---

### Post by maslow788 on 2010-02-16
You are better off using logkeys 

[http://code.google.com/p/logkeys/](http://code.google.com/p/logkeys/)

Great program to log keyboard input on Linux.  Compile, run as root or sudo on Ubuntu 9.10.  

logkeys -s -o /var/log/logkeys.log

---
